enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — RNEXT '=' is a spec-authorized synonym for RNAME when they are identical

evidence.quote: This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.mr_name: CIGAR query-consuming length must equal SEQ length
enforced.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH on the malformed record; silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: SEQ: segment SEQuence. This field can be a `*' when the sequence is not stored. If not a `*', the length of the sequence must equal the sum of lengths of MIS=X operations in CIGAR.

enforced.mr_name: RNEXT '=' alias round-trip invariance under BAM codec
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) after canonical normalization, where P extracts RNEXT field; the '=' alias and explicit RNAME are semantically equivalent per SAMv1 §1.4.7

evidence.quote: This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.mr_name: Mate flag stripping for unpaired reads round-trip invariance
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) after canonical normalization that clears mate-context bits when 0x1 is unset; SAMv1 §1.4 p47: 'If 0x1 is unset, no assumptions can be made about 0x2, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40 and 0x80'

evidence.quote: If 0x1 is unset, no assumptions can be made about 0x2, 0x8, 0x20, 0x40 and 0x80.

enforced.mr_name: RNEXT equals alias invariance under query methods
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method, where T canonicalizes RNEXT between '=' and explicit RNAME forms

evidence.quote: RNEXT: Reference sequence name of the primary alignment of the NEXT read in the template. ... This field is set as `*' when the information is unavailable, and set as `=' if RNEXT is identical RNAME.

enforced.mr_name: Optional tag field permutation invariance under query methods
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method, where T shuffles optional TAG:TYPE:VALUE fields

evidence.quote: As with alignment optional fields (see Section~sec:alnaux), you can freely add new tags for further data fields.

enforced.mr_name: CIGAR split/merge invariance under query methods
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method, where T splits or merges adjacent identical CIGAR ops preserving total consumed length

evidence.quote: Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

enforced.mr_name: Header @CO comment shuffle invariance under query methods
enforced.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method, where T shuffles @CO comment lines

evidence.quote: As with alignment optional fields (see Section~sec:alnaux), you can freely add new tags for further data fields.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM optional tag field order invariance on alignment records
quarantine.oracle: After permuting the order of optional TAG:TYPE:VALUE fields on each alignment line, the parsed record's biological semantics are unchanged. The canonical normalizer sorts optional tags, so the oracle passes for conformant parsers.

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs inside the @HD header line, the parsed header semantics are unchanged. The canonical normalizer sorts subtags within each header line, so the oracle passes for conformant parsers.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs inside each @SQ header line, the parsed reference sequence dictionary semantics are unchanged. The canonical normalizer sorts subtags within each header line, so the oracle passes for conformant parsers.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs inside each @RG header line, the parsed read-group semantics are unchanged. The canonical normalizer sorts subtags within each header line, so the oracle passes for conformant parsers.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the TAG:VALUE pairs inside each @PG header line, the parsed program record semantics are unchanged. The canonical normalizer sorts subtags within each header line, so the oracle passes for conformant parsers.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.oracle: After shuffling the order of @CO comment lines, the parsed header semantics are unchanged. @CO lines are free-text comments with no ordering semantics; the canonical normalizer sorts them, so the oracle passes for conformant parsers.

evidence.quote: In the header, each line is TAB-delimited and, apart from @CO lines, each data field follows a format `TAG:VALUE' where TAG is a two-character string that defines the format and content of VALUE. Thus header lines match /^@(HD|SQ|RG|PG)(\t[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9]:[ -~]+)+$/ or /^@CO\t.*/.

ambiguity_flags: @CO lines are explicitly excluded from the TAG:VALUE format, implying they are free-text comments with no structured ordering semantics, though the spec does not explicitly state 'order is not significant' for @CO lines as it does for other header lines.

quarantine.oracle: After reordering @SQ header lines, the parsed reference sequence dictionary semantics are unchanged. The canonical normalizer sorts @SQ lines by SN, so the oracle passes for conformant parsers. Note: coordinate-sorted alignments reference @SQ order for sort key ordering, but the biological semantics of individual records are unchanged.

evidence.quote: Within each (non-@CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly state that @SQ lines can be in any order relative to each other; however, @SQ order defines the coordinate sort key ordering. For unsorted/queryname-sorted files, @SQ order has no semantic impact on alignment interpretation.

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) — SEQ case is not semantically significant per SAM spec regex

evidence.quote: Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

ambiguity_flags: SAMv1 §1.4.10 regex permits both upper and lower case in SEQ; no normative statement mandates case preservation, but parsers may differ in how they handle case

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR adjacent op split/merge normalization invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each alignment record: splitting 10M into 4M6M or merging 4M6M into 10M preserves the total query-consumed length and reference-consumed length. The parsed CIGAR's semantic effect (total consumed bases) must be identical.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'should' (ADVISORY) not 'must' for adjacent CIGAR ops being different, so some parsers may not normalize this.

quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each alignment record with FLAG 0x4 set: MAPQ is normalized to 255 (not available). The parsed MAPQ value must be 255 regardless of the original value.

evidence.quote: A value 255 indicates that the mapping quality is not available.
A value 255 indicates that the mapping quality is not available.

ambiguity_flags: Bit 0x4 being set means 'no assumptions can be made about MAPQ', so some parsers may preserve the original MAPQ value rather than normalizing to 255.

quarantine.mr_name: CIGAR zero-length op removal normalization invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each alignment record: zero-length CIGAR operations are semantically meaningless and their removal (with merging of adjacent same-type ops) preserves the alignment semantics. The parsed CIGAR's total consumed query and reference bases must be identical.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec does not explicitly forbid zero-length CIGAR ops; the recommendation is ADVISORY level. Some parsers may reject or preserve them differently.

quarantine.mr_name: FLAG 0x4 unmapped bit set on mapped record with RNAME and POS
quarantine.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH on the contradictory flag/RNAME/POS combination; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p137
evidence.quote: If all segments in a template are unmapped, their RNAME should be set as `*' and POS as 0.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'no assumptions can be made' rather than a hard MUST NOT, but the Recommended Practice explicitly says unmapped reads should have RNAME='*' and POS=0, making a mapped RNAME/POS with 0x4 set a clear contradiction

quarantine.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH on the illegal type character; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: All optional fields follow the TAG:TYPE:VALUE format where TAG is a two-character string that matches /[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9]/. In an optional field, TYPE is a single case-sensitive letter which defines the format of VALUE:

ambiguity_flags: The spec defines valid TYPE letters (A, i, f, Z, H, B) but does not explicitly say 'MUST NOT use other letters' — however the format definition is normative and parsers must reject unknown type characters

quarantine.mr_name: TLEN sign consistency violation for paired-end reads
quarantine.oracle: error_consensus: all SUTs must REJECT or CRASH on same-signed TLEN values for paired reads; silent acceptance is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: The intention of this field is to indicate where the other end of the template has been aligned without needing to read the remainder of the SAM file. Unfortunately there has been no clear consensus on the definitions of the template mapped start and end. Thus the exact definitions are implementation-defined.

ambiguity_flags: The spec explicitly says TLEN definitions are 'implementation-defined', so the sign convention may vary across implementations. However, within a single file, paired reads of the same template must have consistent opposite-signed TLEN values — the mutator breaks this internal consistency.

quarantine.mr_name: POS shift with SQ LN bound check preserves coordinate indexing
quarantine.oracle: After shifting POS by +N and increasing @SQ LN by +N, every alignment record's POS + sum of reference-consuming CIGAR ops (M,=,X,D,N) must be <= the new LN. Records with POS=0 or RNAME='*' are skipped. The invariant is that the coordinate system remains 1-based and all positions stay within the valid [1, LN] range.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p138
evidence.quote: * If POS plus the sum of lengths of M=XDN operations in CIGAR exceeds the length specified in the LN field of the @SQ header line (if exists) with an SN equal to RNAME, the alignment should be unmapped, unless the reference sequence is circular (see below).
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'should be unmapped' (advisory, not mandatory) and allows exceptions for circular references. The transform's bound-check no-ops when POS+CIGAR_ref_consume would exceed LN, so it only applies when the invariant holds.

quarantine.oracle: After SAM→BAM→SAM round-trip, every alignment record's POS, RNAME, and CIGAR fields remain identical to the original. The 1-based coordinate system is preserved through binary encoding/decoding. POS=0 (unmapped) and POS>=1 (mapped) values are unchanged.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p137
evidence.quote: * If all segments in a template are unmapped, their RNAME should be set as `*' and POS as 0.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: BAM internally stores POS as 0-based (POS-1) and converts back to 1-based on read. The round-trip tests that this 0-based↔1-based conversion is lossless.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM CRAM codec round-trip preserves alignment content (cram-safe mode)
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) after canonical normalization in cram_safe mode (accounting for =/X collapse to M and NM/MD recomputation per Bonfield CRAM 3.1)

evidence.quote: Sum of lengths of the MIS=X operations shall equal the length of SEQ.

ambiguity_flags: CRAM may collapse =/X CIGAR operators to M by default; the canonical normalizer's cram_safe mode accounts for this lossy edge

quarantine.mr_name: HD VN minor version bump round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) after canonical normalization; toggling between SAM v1.5 and v1.6 is backwards-compatible per SAM version history

evidence.quote: @HD & File-level metadata. Optional. If present, there must be only one @HD line and it must be the first line of the file.

ambiguity_flags: SAM v1.5 -> v1.6 added clarifications but kept backwards-compatibility for files not using 1.6-only features

quarantine.mr_name: SEQ case normalization invariance under query methods
quarantine.oracle: P(parse(x)) == P(parse(T(x))) for each listed method, where T uppercases SEQ field

evidence.quote: Each alignment line has 11 mandatory fields for essential alignment information such as mapping position, and variable number of optional fields for flexible or aligner specific information.

ambiguity_flags: SEQ letter case: 'No assumptions can be made on the letter cases' per SAMv1 §1.4.10, so case changes are semantics-preserving
